annotation_items.text: one and half or maybe two

annotation_items.text: when I'm speaking before I have to think

annotation_items.text: or you have lack of it after one, two, three months there

annotation_items.explanation: 'have lack of' is an incorrect collocation; use 'lack' without 'have' ('a lack of') or rephrase ('stop having it', 'it disappeared'). Also the auxiliary should match the past context ('did you...').

annotation_items.text: that I came take from my lesson

Redundant reflexive 'myself' after 'me' is unnecessary; use 'He means me.'

annotation_items.text: explain English words in English words
annotation_items.explanation: Redundant repetition of 'words' at the end; say 'in English' or 'using English'.

annotation_items.text: suggest you ways to pronounce it to say

annotation_items.explanation: Incorrect word order ('That exists...') and awkward phrase 'such kind of words' — use 'there is' and 'such words' or 'this kind of word'.

annotation_items.text: take English tutor during this year

annotation_items.explanation: Missing article 'an' before 'English tutor' and unnatural use of 'during' — use 'this year' or 'during the year'.
English requires an article with 'platform' in this context: 'on the platform' or 'on a platform'.

'2022 year' is redundant/incorrect in English; use 'in 2022' or 'in the year 2022'.

annotation_items.correction: were closed / were supposed to be closed
annotation_items.explanation: For a past event, 'should be closed' is the wrong modal/tense; use past passive ('were closed') or 'were supposed to be closed'.
Missing definite article 'the' before 'company' when specifying its name.
English orders this as 'the word "remember"' rather than 'the remember word' (unnatural collocation).
After 'do you know' you need an indirect question word order ('what recall means'). The direct question form would be 'what does "recall" mean?'.

annotation_items.correction: call you back / recall a message
annotation_items.explanation: 'Call back' normally takes a person as object ('call you back'), not 'message'; to talk about withdrawing a message use 'recall a message'.
Redundant and ungrammatical word order — simplify to 'when you recall'.

annotation_items.correction: some time ago / a few times ago / a few years ago

Missing definite article before 'company'.

Refers to plural 'lessons' so pronoun and tense should match ('them'); present perfect is more natural for unused items still available.

Amounts are treated as singular ('is'), and 'alive' is an unnatural collocation for money; use 'available' or 'there'.
'Today's' is a possessive determiner and must modify a noun; the clause is a fragment — supply the noun (e.g. 'pitch').
'such kind of' is an unnatural collocation and is missing a noun; use 'that/this kind of' + noun (e.g. 'that kind of visit').
'Discussion' is a count noun here and requires an article: 'a discussion'.

'Optical' is the standard adjective collocation with 'stores' (not 'optic').
Use the definite article 'the' before 'presentation' in this context.
Use the adjective 'northern' to describe a region (not 'north' alone).
